title: Oncidium peruvianoides
taxonomy: Oncidium peruvianoides is classified within the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This species falls under the subfamily Cymbidioideae. It is further categorized within the tribe Maxillarieae and the subtribe Maxillariinae. Oncidium peruvianoides is a member of the genus Oncidium, which comprises several species of orchids. The specific classification of Oncidium peruvianoides highlights its unique characteristics and relationships within the orchid family.

intro: Oncidium peruvianoides, commonly known as the Peruvian Orchid, is a species of flowering orchid native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can aid in wound healing and the treatment of various infections. The bioactive constituents of Oncidium peruvianoides include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Oncidium peruvianoides include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of respiratory and gastrointestinal issues.
